name: Nebius Group N.V.

profile.description: Nebius Group N.V., a technology company, engages in building full-stack infrastructure to service the global AI industry in the United States, the United Kingdom, and internationally. It offers Nebius builds full-stack infrastructure for AI, including large-scale GPU clusters, cloud platforms, and tools and services for developers. The company also provides TripleTen, an edtech platform for re-skilling individuals for careers in technolgy; and Avride, which develops autonomous driving technology for self-driving cars and delivery robotics. It has a strategic alliance with NVIDIA Corporation to support the expansion of the company's AI cloud infrastructure. The company was formerly known as Yandex N.V. and changed its name to Nebius Group N.V. in August 2024. Nebius Group N.V. was founded in 1989 and is based in Schiphol, the Netherlands.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (3/3): Nebius (formerly Yandex's AI division) has executed one of the most transformative pivots in tech — from Russian search engine subsidiary to global AI infrastructure provider, achieving 684% YoY revenue growth to $399M in Q1 2026 [1]. The $27B 5-year Meta partnership and NVIDIA's $2B strategic investment validate the platform at hyperscale. AI Deployment (2/2): Nebius IS an AI company — its entire business is AI cloud infrastructure, and Q1 revenue of $399M is 100% AI-derived [1]. Market Share (2/2): Rapidly gaining share in AI cloud infrastructure with 3.5+ GW secured, competing alongside AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud for hyperscaler and enterprise AI workloads [1]. Product Pipeline (1/2): Full-year 2026 guidance of $3.0-3.4B implies massive scaling; new 1.2 GW Pennsylvania site announced; capex of $20-25B signals continued aggressive expansion [1]. R&D Intensity (1/1): Heavy investment in AI infrastructure software stack and GPU cluster optimization.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (2/4): Nebius's purpose of building the world's most advanced AI infrastructure is specific and forward-looking, though primarily technology-driven rather than humanistic. Community Impact (1/2): Creating domestic AI infrastructure capacity and high-tech jobs in the US (Pennsylvania facility). Employee Retention (1/2): Strong technical team with AI expertise from Yandex heritage. Insider Buying (1/2): NVIDIA's $2B investment provides external validation.

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored. Q1 2026 revenue of $399M surged 684% YoY with adjusted EBITDA of $129.5M versus a loss of $53.7M a year earlier [1]. Full-year guidance of $3.0-3.4B with ~40% EBITDA margins implies exceptional scaling. The $27B Meta partnership provides multi-year revenue visibility. Capex of $20-25B is massive but backed by contracted demand. Transitioning from hypergrowth investment to profitable scale.

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (9/9): Nebius IS AI infrastructure — the company builds and operates AI cloud compute capacity [1]. AI disruption risk is zero because the company IS the AI infrastructure that others depend on. Increasing AI adoption directly increases demand for Nebius's services. The 3.5+ GW of secured power capacity and Meta/NVIDIA partnerships create structural advantages. AI Opportunity (1/1): 100% of revenue ($399M Q1, $3.0-3.4B FY2026 guided) is AI infrastructure revenue.

infinite_game.summary: Nebius has demonstrated extraordinary courage in pivoting from Yandex's Russian operations to a global AI infrastructure company, making massive capital commitments ($20-25B capex) backed by hyperscaler partnerships. The transformation is too recent to evaluate multi-decade consistency, but the boldness of the pivot and scale of investment signal genuine infinite game thinking.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (2/3): Building the AI infrastructure backbone for the world's most demanding AI workloads connects to a genuine purpose of democratizing AI compute access. The 684% growth and hyperscaler partnerships validate execution.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): Strong technical team with AI expertise. Rapid scaling requires high trust.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): Competing constructively with AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud in AI infrastructure.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (0/2): The pivot FROM Yandex was transformative, but Nebius as a standalone entity hasn't yet faced this test.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (2/2): Committing $20-25B in capex and pivoting entirely from Yandex's Russian operations to global AI infrastructure required extraordinary courage — betting the company on an emerging market with massive capital requirements [1].

investment_thesis: Nebius delivered Q1 2026 revenue of $399M (+684% YoY) with adjusted EBITDA of $129.5M, executing one of the fastest revenue ramps in tech history [1]. The $27B 5-year Meta partnership, NVIDIA's $2B strategic investment, and 3.5+ GW of secured power capacity provide extraordinary forward visibility. Full-year 2026 guidance of $3.0-3.4B with ~40% EBITDA margins suggests Nebius is building a world-class AI infrastructure franchise at hyperscale.
thesis_continued: Capex guidance of $20-25B for 2026 represents massive capital intensity that requires continued external financing and perfect execution [1]. The AI infrastructure market is rapidly commoditizing as AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, and CoreWeave all aggressively expand GPU capacity. Customer concentration risk is extreme — the $27B Meta partnership likely dominates near-term revenue. Any AI spending slowdown from hyperscalers would devastate Nebius's growth trajectory. The Yandex heritage creates geopolitical complexity.
key_catalysts: Full-year revenue of $3.0-3.4B validating the AI infrastructure scaling thesis with ~40% EBITDA margins
Pennsylvania 1.2 GW facility construction progressing, adding domestic capacity for US-based hyperscaler demand
Additional hyperscaler partnerships beyond Meta diversifying the customer base and reducing concentration risk
key_risks: $20-25B capex requiring massive ongoing financing that dilutes shareholders or increases leverage if AI demand plateaus
Customer concentration — Meta partnership likely representing majority of near-term revenue creates single-customer dependency risk
AI infrastructure commoditization as AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, and CoreWeave all aggressively expand competing GPU capacity
sources: Nebius Group Q1 2026 Earnings Release and Shareholder Letter, May 2026
Yahoo Finance — NBIS Key Statistics, accessed June 2026
